mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases in Kubernetes, providing a seamless experience from deployment to maintenance. It integrates directly with the Kubernetes API for high availability and automated operations. Key trade-offs include reliance on Kubernetes for management and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for CloudNativePG.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the operator.
> Installs the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> Creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres' with 3 instances.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

kubectl describe cluster my-postgres -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Lists the PostgreSQL clusters in the specified namespace.
> Describes the cluster to check its status and details.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters and any data stored in them.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your PostgreSQL data. This step upgrades the operator and verifies the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get pods -n cloudnative-pg

```

> List all clusters to ensure you have backups.
> Upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check resource limits and ensure the cluster configuration is correct.

troubleshooting.title: PostgreSQL cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to incorrect PostgreSQL version specified.

**Fix:**
Verify the PostgreSQL version in the Cluster CRD and correct it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This often indicates an issue with the container startup, such as configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to PostgreSQL database
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service misconfigurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Review the network policies and service configurations to ensure proper access.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore functionalities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg'.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
